name: Hebbian Association Learning

source.author: Donald O. Hebb
source.paper: The Organization of Behavior: A Neuropsychological Theory

biological_basis: When an axon of cell A repeatedly participates in firing cell B, synaptic efficacy increases. Co-activation strengthens connections; uncorrelated activity weakens them over time.
core_claim: Learning is local and associative: neurons that fire together wire together. No external teacher signal required for basic association formation.

implementable_mechanism.description: Weighted graph of concept nodes. When two concepts co-activate in workspace, increment edge weight. Apply decay each cycle to prevent unbounded growth and enable forgetting.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: for (a, b) in pairs(active_concepts): graph[a][b] += lr; graph[b][a] += lr; for edge in graph: edge.weight *= decay; prune(edge.weight < threshold)

name: Complementary Learning Systems

source.author: James L. McClelland, Bruce L. McNaughton, Randall C. O'Reilly
source.paper: Why there are complementary learning systems in the hippocampus and neocortex: Insights from the successes and failures of connectionist models of learning and memory

biological_basis: Hippocampus rapidly encodes episodic memories with sparse representations; neocortex slowly integrates structured knowledge. Fast learning avoids interference; slow learning extracts statistical structure.
core_claim: Two memory systems solve the stability-plasticity dilemma: fast episodic store (hippocampus) + slow semantic store (neocortex). Consolidation transfers knowledge from fast to slow.

implementable_mechanism.description: Fast store (AssociationStore) learns immediately from co-activation. Slow store (BeliefGraph) updates via periodic consolidation replaying episodic traces. Prevents catastrophic forgetting.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: fast.learn(episode); if sleep_phase: for trace in fast.sample(): slow.integrate(trace, small_lr)

notes: Implemented in v2.0: EpisodicBuffer (fast) + BeliefGraph (slow) + SleepReplay.

name: Hippocampal Sleep Replay

source.author: Matthew A. Wilson, Bruce L. McNaughton
source.paper: Reactivation of hippocampal ensemble memories during sleep

biological_basis: During slow-wave sleep, hippocampal place cell ensembles reactivate in temporal sequences matching prior waking experience, driving cortical consolidation.
core_claim: Offline replay of waking experience strengthens memories and transfers them to long-term cortical storage without new sensory input.

implementable_mechanism.description: After training episodes, run offline pass: sample stored episodic sequences and replay through slow learning system with reduced learning rate. Strengthens associations without new input.
implementable_mechanism.pseudocode: for episode in sample(episodes, n=replay_count): for step in episode: slow_store.hebbian_update(step.concepts, lr=small); association_store.reinforce(step.pairs)

notes: Implemented in v2.0 via SleepReplay.run() and agent.sleep().
